item.description:
## Changes
- **Sharpie mode (Rock Salt) prints cleanly.** Capo lines and between-song notes no longer clip: the marker font gets more room, so a title, its capo, and a wrapping note all fit on the page.
- On the song library, **selecting songs now tints the whole row**, using the same color the setlist builder uses to highlight overlapping songs.
- The **sort arrow** in the song library is now visible in every theme.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The **Add / Edit Song dialog** is wider, with the note fields beside the rest so the whole form fits without scrolling. If you close it with unsaved edits, it asks whether to save, discard, or keep editing.
- On the song library, **clicking anywhere on a row selects it** (except its buttons), and the selection bar stays put at the top and follows you as you scroll, so it never jumps the page.
- On printed setlists, metaset subsongs now print at the same size as the between-song notes.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- **Backstage is redesigned** around a floating left sidebar and a wide canvas, so most settings fit without scrolling. Print options move to their own **Print/Export** tab with a live sample preview.
- **Print options are now toggle switches**, with new controls to hide the band name, venue, or date on a sheet. The venue shortens to fit and expands to its full name when there's room.
- **Sharpie mode** defaults to a scrawled marker font (with a cleaner option), in natural title case with the key in parentheses.
- The **song library sorts with a Finder-style arrow** showing the active column and direction, and a new **Licenses menu** lets you view or deactivate this computer from the menu bar.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The print dialog gains two options: **Hide song numbers** for a clean, unnumbered sheet, and **Sharpie mode**, which prints the setlist in a bold handwritten marker font. Both show live in the preview.
- Action buttons now carry their meaning in their color, consistent across every theme: green to save or create, blue to print or export, red to delete, orange to cancel.
- The print preview now shows the trial watermark exactly as it will print, with a note that a Pro or Road Warrior license removes it.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The free trial now marks printed and exported sheets with a light diagonal stamp, the same as the free version. Every feature stays open during the trial; a clean, gig-ready sheet is what a Pro or Road Warrior license unlocks.
- Photo import shows the correct trial allotment: five scans a month, not twenty, and the counter now tracks as you use them.
- The trial banner stays pinned below the top bar as you scroll, and its button reads "Upgrade to keep using all Pro features."
- On the builder, the Print, cue sheet, and delete actions are right-aligned in their row.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The "See the tiers" button on the trial welcome screen now opens the license page as intended.
- Pro and Road Warrior appear on the Account tab as side-by-side cards, each with a short description of what it includes, so the difference reads at a glance.
- Clearer trial banner wording. During the trial you already have every feature, so the banner now points to keeping the full app after the trial rather than unlocking it.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- New installs now get a **free 30-day trial** of the full app: every feature, no watermark, no card. After 30 days, Setcraft settles into the free version, and your library, shows, and setlists always stay on your Mac.
- A small banner shows how many trial days remain, with a one-time welcome that explains how the trial works.
- If a license is close to expiring, Setcraft now warns you beforehand so the full features never switch off unexpectedly. It is dismissable.
- Photo import is fairer: a scan only counts against your monthly allotment when it actually reads something. A blurry photo that comes back empty no longer costs you.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- New pricing model: two one-time tiers. **Pro** ($19.99) includes one artist profile; **Road Warrior** ($34.99) includes unlimited artist profiles. The older "additional artist profile" add-on is retired, and your artist limit now follows your tier.
- Already on Pro and want more bands? Upgrade to Road Warrior from inside the app and pay only the difference, no need to buy again. Your library carries straight over.
- Exporting a portable backup to move your whole library to another Mac is now part of Pro and Road Warrior. Restoring a backup stays free for everyone, and the automatic on-device snapshots are unchanged, so your data is always safe.
- Paid features (watermark-free exports, photo import, Bandsintown sync, the free song cap) now run through one consistent entitlement check.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- When you build a setlist, you can now create a brand-new show right from the start window, without leaving for the Shows page.
- Photo import can now turn a line it doesn't recognize into a new song on the spot. Enter the title and short name, your main artist is filled in for you (editable for covers), and it's added to your library as you import.
- The action buttons on each show in the Shows list are easier to read.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Navigation now holds steady as each page loads, with smooth fades between tabs and a gentle entrance on the Build Setlist screen.
- Fixed updates not always taking full effect, so each new version now loads its latest changes reliably.
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Your settings and one-time prompts now reliably stick between launches.
- Cleaner, steadier launch and navigation, with no flicker or font flash when opening the app or moving between tabs.
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Various small visual fixes and behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Fixed an error that could pop up when saving or printing a setlist (the setlist was actually saved, but the app wrongly reported it had failed). - The Build Setlist tab now opens with a clear prompt to pick an upcoming show or a past setlist, instead of a dimmed builder. - The navigation bar keeps a consistent width across themes, and at smaller window sizes the links move to a tidy second row instead of wrapping unevenly. - Assorted visual polish and behind-the-scenes refinements.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Assorted visual polish and behind-the-scenes refinements.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Assorted visual polish and behind-the-scenes refinements.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Assorted visual polish and behind-the-scenes refinements.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Assorted visual polish and behind-the-scenes refinements.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The Lyric Book and Chart Book buttons now have their own clear descriptions. - Behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- More of your personalization now carries over across app updates and backups. - Tuned one theme's background artwork for better readability. - Behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The Backstage tabs and the Photo Import intro now sit on a solid panel, so they stay readable on every theme. - Behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Several themes now have richer background artwork. - Behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Backstage Preferences now use the full window width, so the layout has more room to breathe. - Behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The downloaded disk image now opens to a tidier install window, with the app and an Applications shortcut laid out for drag-to-install. - Behind-the-scenes polish.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- AirDrop: send a setlist, tech cue sheet, PRO report, lyric or chart book, or a single song's lyrics or chart straight to a nearby Mac, iPhone, or iPad — right from the Print options, without saving a file first. - The PRO Report can now be saved as a PDF (or AirDropped), not just printed.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Fixed an occasional macOS alert "beep" that could sound when typing while no text field was focused.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Your saved look and preferences now persist more reliably across app updates, backups, and a move to a new computer. - Assorted visual polish and behind-the-scenes refinements.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The small "?" help markers are gone. Tooltips now appear when you hover over a label, and a new switch in Backstage (Preferences) turns help tooltips on or off. - Printed setlists and exported PDFs now use one consistent font, regardless of the theme you have on in the app. - Backstage theme tiles are more compact, and the song editor (and other dialogs) open scrolled to the top so the first field is in view.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- New built-in Help Center: open it from the Help menu (Setcraft Help). It opens in its own window with a searchable set of offline guides, from getting started to troubleshooting. - Fixed a case where adding an artist profile showed the upgrade prompt even when you had a paid seat free (the demo band was being counted against your limit).
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Fixed license activation from the first-launch welcome screen, which failed with "Could not reach the activation server" during onboarding. - The onboarding "Pick a look" step keeps its Back and Next buttons in view, and its preview no longer shows non-clickable navigation links. - The top navigation links now show on the dashboard too, and "Backstage" carries a gear icon so its settings purpose reads at a glance. - The Bandsintown sync window links to a short guide for getting your API key, and the upgrade prompts open straight to the Account & License tab. - Backstage: the Primary and Active badges next to a profile name are aligned and evenly spaced.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The demo band's name no longer shows a rename (pencil) button on the dashboard. The demo is a read-only sample, so its name was never editable.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- You can now enter an additional-artist-profile add-on key right on the Account & License tab in Backstage, instead of only inside the Manage Artists dialog. - When you have a paid artist profile you have not created yet, Backstage points it out: a note on the Account & License tab and a small count badge on the Artists tab, so an unused seat does not sit there unnoticed.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The free version is friendlier: limits surface only when you reach one, instead of as persistent banners. Your primary artist profile and the demo band stay available; extra profiles need a license, and you choose which profile is your primary in Backstage. - The demo band is a full sandbox now (45 songs, lyrics, a sample chart, saved reminders). Build full-length setlists and tech cue sheets with it on the free version, with clean, unwatermarked exports. It's read-only, and you can hide it from Backstage instead of deleting it. - Free version: a setlist is capped at 15 songs (the demo is exempt), and Bandsintown sync shows as locked.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The "Saved defaults" help tooltip in the builder's Between-Song Reminders panel no longer runs off the left edge of the window. - Removed a duplicate "View" menu from the menu bar. macOS already provides one with Enter Full Screen, so Reload now lives under the File menu.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The Tech Cue Sheet's "Back to Setlist" link returns to the setlist builder, on the set you were working on, instead of the read-only show view. - "Edit Setlist" on the show view opens that setlist in the builder ready to edit, instead of opening an empty builder. - In-app email links (beta feedback, the Backstage links, and the Licenses page contact) now open your mail client.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- In the setlist builder, the Between-Song Reminders panel moved into the song library column, so saving a reminder no longer shrinks the room for the setlist above it. The reminders and metaset panels are now collapsible, and a saved reminder can be added to the current set with a single click. - The saved-setlist bar gained a Tech Cue Sheet button, next to Print / PDF.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- The printed Tech Cue Sheet now starts each set on its own page, instead of packing the next set below the previous one, and a single song's row no longer splits across a page break.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Editing a metaset's master song no longer drops its members from the builder. - The PRO Report opens the native print dialog, with a preview and save-as-PDF, instead of doing nothing or opening a browser window. - The printed Tech Cue Sheet uses a larger, more readable font.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Tech Cue Sheets now show the songs inside a medley, each with its own FOH and Lighting notes. - The cue sheet's back link returns to the setlist instead of a blank page. - The PRO Report opens in its own window with a working Print button. - Report a Bug now writes to hello@getsetcraft.com.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Privacy Policy and Terms of Service are now reachable from the Help menu and from Backstage.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Internal licensing cleanup: the app is now built entirely from permissive open-source components, and ships a smaller download as a result. - Added an Open-Source Licenses page (About panel, Help menu, and Backstage).
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## What's new
**1.2.4**
- Help > About now opens the About panel. - Removed the unused macOS window tab bar.
**1.2.3**
- Tutorial wording fixes: exploring the demo band, cover songs, and backups.
**1.2.2**
- Lyric and chart books now print, not just export to PDF. - Photo Import no longer shows "Setlist created" before you import anything. - Account & License shows your license status and activated artist profiles. - The Shows list gains a PRO Report link, and the tutorials were reviewed for accuracy with new walkthroughs.
**1.2.1**
- Title-cased the navigation and page labels.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Tutorial wording fixes: clearer guidance on exploring the demo band, a corrected note about cover songs, and a clearer Backup walkthrough.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Lyric and chart books now print, not just export to PDF. - Photo Import no longer shows "Setlist created" before you import anything. - Account & License shows your license status and activated artist profiles. - The Shows list gains a PRO Report link on each show with a setlist. - Tutorials reviewed for accuracy, with new walkthroughs for lyrics and charts, show filtering, saving, and a Backup & Restore tour.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## Changes
- Navigation and page labels now read in consistent title case (Build Setlist, Photo Import), so they display correctly on themes whose fonts are not all-caps.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.

## New
- **Automatic updates.** Setcraft now checks for new versions on its own and can install them for you, so you stay current without downloading and dragging a new copy by hand. Check anytime from Help, Check for Updates.
Includes everything from 1.1.0 (Local Backup, the Backstage reorganization, and the accessibility and copy pass) for anyone coming from an earlier build.
Requires macOS 12 or later on Apple Silicon.
